page: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is a GPU-accelerated cloud server featuring two NVIDIA L40S GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined 96 GB of VRAM. The system is powered by an Intel Xeon 6448H processor with 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. It includes 375.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 11.72 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a 2200 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieved a strong multi-core CPU score of 62,892.75 in stress-ng and a Passmark CPU score of 64,768.69. Memory read bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 4,985.78 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 211.9 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Single-threaded performance is highlighted by top-tier gzip compression at 35.41 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 15.2 GB/sec. For artificial intelligence workloads, the dual L40S GPUs deliver top-tier LLM inference speeds, processing 6,275.36 tokens per second for prompt processing and 141.42 tokens per second for text generation on a medium 7B model.

From a resource density perspective, the server offers a high-capacity hardware profile that balances massive GPU memory with substantial system RAM and fast NVMe storage. However, the CPU allocation is shared, and single-core performance is average, as shown by the stress-ng single-core score of 2,209.29. This makes the instance highly cost-efficient for parallelized, GPU-bound tasks rather than single-threaded CPU operations. The server is optimally configured for workloads such as large language model inference, deep learning training, high-throughput database operations, and data-intensive scientific computing.
description: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is a high-density GPU-accelerated cloud server designed for parallel computing and artificial intelligence workloads. It features two NVIDIA L40S Ada Lovelace GPUs providing a total of 96 GB VRAM, paired with an Intel Xeon 6448H processor containing 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs. The hardware configuration includes 375.0 GB of system memory and 2200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in LLM inference, reaching 6,275.36 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 7B model, alongside strong multi-core CPU and memory read bandwidth capabilities. While single-core CPU performance is average, the server's massive GPU and memory resources make it highly efficient for deep learning, large-scale database operations, and high-throughput data processing.
og_description: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is a GPU-accelerated server featuring two NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 96 GB VRAM, 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 375 GB RAM, and a 2200 GB NVMe SSD for AI and database workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-l40s-32c-375g-96vram is a GPU server with 2x NVIDIA L40S GPUs, 32 vCPUs, 375 GB RAM, and 2200 GB NVMe storage for AI and database tasks.
tagline: A high-density GPU server combining dual Ada Lovelace accelerators with massive memory and NVMe storage for demanding AI inference workloads.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 96 GB total VRAM for parallel acceleration
32 Intel Xeon physical cores and 375 GB of system memory
2200 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier LLM inference performance for small and medium models
Strong multi-core CPU and memory read bandwidth benchmarks
Optimized for AI model deployment, deep learning, and database operations
